summary: The test suite covers core functionality (manifest parsing, license checking, report generation, error handling) and follows the requested TDD approach with clear organization. However, rigor is weak: it tests primarily happy path and basic error cases but lacks edge case coverage (malformed JSON, special version operators, empty manifests, license matching edge cases). The design is readable with good test naming and TDD iteration comments, but integration tests are complex; fixture files are referenced but not defined in the provided code, making it impossible to verify they support all test assertions. The suite would catch major functionality breaks and workflow structure issues, but would likely miss subtle bugs in parsing logic (sed/jq operations), version normalization, and license matching. No explicit validation of shell script quality (shellcheck, bash -n syntax, shebang).
